title: Messaging Outcomes, Delivery Claims, and Evidence · Mastery Check
summary: A production review compares near-miss TypeScript messaging decisions for messaging outcomes, delivery claims, and evidence and requires the smallest defensible messaging outcome, delivery claim, and evidence charter with changed-case evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for messaging outcome, delivery claim, and evidence charter. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define producer, consumer, operator, security, accessibility, latency, loss, duplicate, and recovery evidence for one messaging outcome.
Distinguish commands, events, notifications, documents, and queries by ownership, audience, expected handling, and change semantics.
State at-most-once, at-least-once, effectively-once effect, ordering, and durability claims without promising impossible end-to-end exactly-once delivery.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Outcome Evidence Contract · changed case · Quiz Outcome Evidence Contract
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz messaging team handles case 5274 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to separate local publish, broker ownership, and observable stakeholder effect; preserve one tenant fairness invariant; then lose a quorum member and recompute majority availability. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Define producer, consumer, operator, security, accessibility, latency, loss, duplicate, and recovery evidence for one messaging outcome.
